meaning: adj.由气质引起的,心情变化快的,喜怒无常的
examples: He is very temperamental and critical.
The boys couldn't start the temperamental motor.
They are firm friends in spite of temperamental differences.
They are less likely to understand the emotional and temperamental issues that go on with investing.
In 2003, a year before the previous election, Labor installed the temperamental Mark Latham as leader.
